en.extract:
The varied tit (Sittiparus varius) is a perching bird from the tit family, Paridae.  It occurs in the eastern Palearctic in Japan, Korea, and locally in northeastern China (southern Liaoning) and extreme southeastern Russia (southern Kurile Islands).


Taxonomy
The varied tit was described by the Dutch zoologist Coenraad Jacob Temminck and the German ornithologist Hermann Schlegel in 1845 and given the binomial name Parus varius. Until the end of the twentieth century the varied tit was usually placed along with most of the other member of the tit family in the genus Parus. In 2005 the report of a molecular phylogenetic study that examined mitochondrial DNA sequences from members of the tit family, recommended including the varied tit along with around fifteen other species in the resurrected genus Poecile. The authors of a 2013 study that analysed both mitochondrial and nuclear sequences, suggested splitting Poecile and placing the varied tit and its sister taxon, the white-fronted tit in their own genus Sittiparus. The genus had originally been erected by the Belgium politician and naturalist Edmond de Sélys Longchamps in 1884 with the varied tit as the type species. This proposal was adopted by the International Ornithologists' Union.
Owston's tit (Sittiparus owstoni), the Iriomote tit (Sittiparus olivaceus) and the chestnut-bellied tit (Sittiparus castaneoventris) were formerly considered as subspecies of the varied tit, but were promoted to species status following the publication of a phylogenetic study in 2014.
A number of subspecies have been described, of which one has become extinct in the 20th century:

S. v. varius (Temminck & Schlegel, 1845) - southern Kuriles (Kunashir, Shikotan, Iturup), north-eastern China, central and southern Korea, Japan
S. v. sunsunpi (Kuroda, 1919) - Tanegashima
S. v. namiyei (Kuroda, 1918) - Toshima Island, Niijima and Kōzu-shima
S. v. yakushimensis (Kuroda, 1919) - Yakushima
S. v. amamii Kuroda, 1922 - Amami Ōshima, Tokunoshima and Okinawa Island
S. v. orii Kuroda, 1923 Daito varied tit - formerly Daitō Islands (Kitadaitōjima and Minami Daitōjima), extinct c. 1940


Description
The varied tit is 12–14 cm (4.7–5.5 in) long and weighs 16–18 g (0.56–0.63 oz). The wing length is 6.0–7.8 cm (2.4–3.1 in). In the nominate race S. v. varius the crown, the bill, the throat, the upper breast and the nape are black.  Forehead, face, and cheeks are white. Back, wings, and tail are bluish grey.  Mantle, lower breast, belly and undertail coverts are chestnut coloured. From the crown to the nape runs a thin white central line. The feet are dark grey.
Its habitat consists of open mixed forests, in particular with the Japanese Castanopsis species Castanopsis cuspidata and Japanese larch, coniferous forests with Japanese yews, Cryptomeria (sugi), and pines as well as bamboo forests at mountain slopes and in river valleys.
The varied tit eats a mixed diet consisting of seeds and insects, namely caterpillars.
Its call consists of tiny tones which sounds like tzu....tzu....tzu...


In culture
In Japan varied tits have been used in a kind of show called "omikujihiki" to draw fortune telling-slips (omikuji). Using a small model Shinto shrine the birds will deposit a coin, ring a bell, open the door, remove a fortune telling slip and return with it. They may also use their beaks to open it by cutting a string or tape.


Notes


References

del Hoyo, J., Elliot, A., & Christie D. (eds). (2007). Handbook of the Birds of the World. Volume 12: Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions. ISBN 978-84-96553-42-2


External links

Northern Region Water Resources Office, Taiwan: Birds of Shihmen Reservoir Archived 2007-03-24 at the Wayback Machine. Retrieved 2006-NOV-21.
Oriental Bird Club: Varied tit photos. Retrieved 2006-NOV-21.
Endangered animals of Russia: Тиссовая синица (Tissovaya sinitsa, Varied Tit) Archived 2000-08-19 at the Wayback Machine [in Russian]. Retrieved 2006-NOV-21.

The '''varied tit''' ('''''Sittiparus varius''''') is a perching bird from the [[tit (bird)|tit]] [[Family (taxonomy)|family]], Paridae.  It occurs in the eastern [[Palearctic]] in [[Japan]], [[Korea]], and locally in northeastern [[China]] (southern [[Liaoning]]) and extreme southeastern [[Russia]] (southern [[Kurile Islands]]).

==Taxonomy==
The varied tit was [[Species description|described]] by the Dutch zoologist [[Coenraad Jacob Temminck]] and the German ornithologist [[Hermann Schlegel]] in 1845 and given the [[binomial name]] ''Parus varius''.<ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Siebold | editor-first=Philipp Franz von | editor-link=Philipp Franz von Siebold | last1=Temminck  | first1=Coenraad Jacob | author1-link=Coenraad Jacob Temminck | last2=Schlegel | first2=Hermann  | author2-link=Hermann Schlegel | year=1845 | title=Fauna Japonica | volume=4: Aves | pages=71–72, [https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/53188923 plate 35] | publisher=Regis Auspiciis | place=Lugduni Batavorum (Leiden) | url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/53188751 }} Title page dated 1850</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=12 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | pages=117–119 | url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482234 }}</ref>{{efn|''Fauna Japonica'' was issued in fascicles (parts). Although the description of the varied tit on pages 71-72 was included in fascicles IV-VIII which were published in 1848,<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Sherborn | first1=C. Davies | last2=Jentink | first2=F.A. | year=1895 | title=On the dates of the parts of Siebold's 'Fauna Japonica' and Giebel's 'Allgemeine Zoologie' (first edition) | pages=149–150 | publisher=Academic Press, [etc.] |journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London  | url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/30983080 }}</ref> plate 35 was published with fascicle 2 in July 1845.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Morioka | first1=H. | last2=Dickinson | first2=E.C. | last3=Hiraoka | first3=T. | last4=Allen | first4=D. | last5=Yamasaki | first5=T. | year=2005 | title=Types of Japanese birds | journal=National Science Museum Monographs (Tokyo) | volume=28 | pages=1–154 [130] | url=http://ci.nii.ac.jp/els/110004665755.pdf?id=ART0007395376&type=pdf&lang=en&host=cinii&order_no=&ppv_type=&lang_sw=&no=1493156712&cp= }}</ref>}} Until the end of the twentieth century the varied tit was usually placed along with most of the other member of the tit family in the genus ''[[Parus]]''. In 2005 the report of a [[molecular phylogenetic]] study that examined mitochondrial DNA sequences from members of the tit family, recommended including the varied tit along with around fifteen other species in the resurrected genus ''[[Poecile]]''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Gill | first1=F.B. | last2=Slikas | first2=B. | last3=Sheldon | first3=F.H. | year=2005 | title=Phylogeny of titmice (Paridae): II. Species relationships based on sequences of the mitochondrial cytochrome-''b'' gene | journal=Auk | volume=122 | pages=121–143 | doi=10.1642/0004-8038(2005)122[0121:POTPIS]2.0.CO;2 | s2cid=86067032 }}</ref> The authors of a 2013 study that analysed both mitochondrial and nuclear sequences, suggested splitting ''Poecile'' and placing the varied tit and its sister taxon, the [[white-fronted tit]] in their own genus ''Sittiparus''. The genus had originally been erected by the Belgium politician and naturalist [[Edmond de Sélys Longchamps]] in 1884 with the varied tit as the [[type species]].<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Selys-Longchamps | first=Edmund de | author-link=Edmond de Sélys Longchamps | year=1884 | title=Considérations sur le genre mélange (''Parus'') | language=fr | journal=Bulletin de la Société Zoologique de France | volume=9 | pages=32–78 [58] | url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/35675531 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=12 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=71 | url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482188 }}</ref> This proposal was adopted by the [[International Ornithologists' Union]].<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Johansson  | first1=U.S. | last2=Ekman | first2=J. | last3=Bowie | first3=R.C.K. | last4=Halvarsson | first4=P. | last5=Ohlson | first5=J.I. | last6=Price | first6=T.D. | last7=Ericson | first7=P.G.P. | year=2013 | title=A complete multilocus species phylogeny of the tits and chickadees (Aves: Paridae) | journal=Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution | volume=69 | issue=3 | pages=852–860 | doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2013.06.019 | pmid=23831453}}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | title=Waxwings and their allies, tits & penduline tits | work=World Bird List Version 6.1 | url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/waxwings/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union| access-date= 15 February 2016 }}</ref>

[[Owston's tit]] (''Sittiparus owstoni''), the [[Iriomote tit]] (''Sittiparus olivaceus'') and the [[chestnut-bellied tit]] (''Sittiparus castaneoventris'') were formerly considered as subspecies of the varied tit, but were promoted to species status following the publication of a [[phylogenetic]] study in 2014.<ref name=ioc/><ref name=mckay>{{ cite journal | last1=McKay  | first1=B.D. | last2=Mays Jnr | first2=H.L. | last3=Tao | first3=C.-T. | last4=Wan | first4=D. | last5=Higuchi | first5=H. | last6=Nishium | first6=I. | year=2014 | title=Incorporating color into integrative taxonomy: analysis of the Varied Tit (''Sittiparus varius'') complex in East Asia | journal=Systems Biology | volume=63 | issue=4 | pages=505–517 | doi=10.1093/sysbio/syu016 | pmid=24603127| doi-access=free }}</ref>

A number of [[subspecies]] have been described, of which one has become [[extinct]] in the 20th century:<ref name=ioc/>

* ''S. v. varius'' (Temminck & Schlegel, 1845) - southern [[Kuriles]] ([[Kunashir]], [[Shikotan]], [[Iturup]]), north-eastern [[China]], central and southern [[Korea]], [[Japan]]
*''S. v. sunsunpi'' ([[Nagamichi Kuroda|Kuroda]], 1919) - [[Tanegashima]]
*''S. v. namiyei'' (Kuroda, 1918) - [[Toshima Island]], [[Niijima]] and [[Kōzu-shima]]
*''S. v. yakushimensis'' (Kuroda, 1919) - [[Yakushima]]
*''S. v. amamii'' Kuroda, 1922 - [[Amami Ōshima]], [[Tokunoshima]] and [[Okinawa Island]]
*''S. v. orii'' Kuroda, 1923 [[Daito varied tit]] - formerly [[Daitō Islands]] ([[Kitadaitōjima]] and [[Minami Daitōjima]]), extinct c. 1940

== Description ==
The varied tit is {{convert|12|–|14|cm|in|abbr=on}} long and weighs {{convert|16|–|18|g|oz|abbr=on}}. The wing length is {{convert|6.0|–|7.8|cm|in|abbr=on}}. In the [[nominate race]] ''S. v. varius'' the crown, the bill, the throat, the upper breast and the nape are black.  Forehead, face, and cheeks are white. Back, wings, and tail are bluish grey.  Mantle, lower breast, belly and undertail coverts are chestnut coloured. From the crown to the nape runs a thin white central line. The feet are dark grey.

Its habitat consists of open mixed forests, in particular with the Japanese ''[[Castanopsis]]'' species ''[[Castanopsis cuspidata]]'' and [[Japanese larch]], coniferous forests with [[Taxus cuspidata|Japanese yew]]s, [[Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]], and [[pine]]s as well as [[bamboo]] forests at mountain slopes and in river valleys.

The varied tit eats a mixed diet consisting of seeds and insects, namely caterpillars.

Its call consists of tiny tones which sounds like ''tzu....tzu....tzu...''

==In culture==
In Japan varied tits have been used in a kind of show called "omikujihiki" to draw [[o-mikuji|fortune telling-slips (omikuji)]]. Using a small model [[Shinto shrine]] the birds will deposit a coin, ring a bell, open the door, remove a fortune telling slip and return with it. They may also use their beaks to open it by cutting a string or tape.<ref>[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nYDa9gK7TGQ ヤマガラの芸ーその4 おみくじ芸 (Yamagara Bird Tricks Part4) June 12, 2010] Retrieved March 24, 2017</ref><ref>Yamaguchi, Noriyaki [http://www.bird-research.jp/1_shiryo/seitai/yamagara.pdf Varied Tit Yamagara (Jpn) Poecile varius December 14, 2005] ''Bird Research News'' Vol.2 No. 12 Retrieved March 24, 2017</ref>

zh.extract:
杂色山雀（学名：Sittiparus varius），为山雀科赤腹山雀属的鸟类。分布于朝鲜、日本、中国大陆的辽宁和俄罗斯东南部的千島群島等地，主要生活于森林中。该物种的模式产地在日本本州。


分類
雜色山雀由荷蘭動物學家康拉德·雅各·特明克與德國鳥類學家赫爾曼·施萊格爾於1845年描述，並命名為Parus varius。 直到20世紀末，雜色山雀通常與大多數其他山雀科成員一起被歸入山雀屬（Parus）。2005年，研究山雀科成員線粒體DNA序列的分子系統發生學研究報告，建議將雜色山雀與其他大約15個物種歸入重新恢復的高山山雀屬（Poecile）。 2013年的一項研究分析了線粒體和核序列，建議拆分高山山雀屬，並將雜色山雀及其姐妹物種白頭山雀歸入他們自己的屬赤腹山雀屬。該屬最初由比利時政治家兼自然學家愛德蒙·德·塞利斯·龍尚於1884年建立，並將雜色山雀作為模式種。 此提案已被世界鳥類學家聯合會（IOU）採納。
奧氏山雀（Sittiparus owstoni）、西表山雀（Sittiparus olivaceus）和赤腹山雀（Sittiparus castaneoventris）以前被認為是雜色山雀的亞種，但在2014年系統發生學研究發表後被提升為物種。
許多亞種已被描述，其中一個亞種在20世紀滅絕：  

S. v. varius （Temminck & Schlegel, 1845） - 南部千島群島（國後島、色丹島、擇捉島）、中國東北、韓國中部和南部、日本
S. v. sunsunpi （黑田長禮, 1919） -種子島
S. v. namiyei （黑田長禮, 1918） -利島、新島和神津島
S. v. yakushimensis （黑田長禮, 1919） -屋久島
S. v. amamii 黑田長禮, 1922 -奄美大島、德之島和沖繩島
大東雜色山雀 S. v. orii 黑田長禮, 1923 - 原分佈於大東群島（北大東島和南大東島），約於1940年滅絕


描述
杂色山雀体长约 12–14 cm (4.7–5.5 in)，重量约 16–18 g (0.56–0.63 oz)。翅膀长度约 6.0–7.8 cm (2.4–3.1 in)。指名亚种(v.varius)其头顶、喙、喉咙、前胸上半部、和颈背都是黑色的，后头中央有一细长白斑。额头，脸和脸颊是白色的。背部，翅膀和尾巴呈蓝灰色。后颈、胸、腹及两胁栗红色，胸部和腹部的中央至尾下覆羽淡黄褐色。脚是深灰色的。
其栖息地包括各种开放林地，特别是日本栲树（Castanopsis cuspidata）和日本落叶松（Japanese larch）组成的杂木林、日本红豆杉（Cryptomeria（sugi））与松树组成的针叶林、遍布竹林的山坡地、以及河谷等。
主要以昆虫和昆虫幼虫为食，也吃植物果实和种子。
叫声由短促的音调组成，听起来像 tzu...tzu...tzu ...


文化中的地位
在日本，雜色山雀曾用於一種名為「御神籤曳」的表演中，來抽取御神籤。使用一個小型的神社模型，鳥兒會投下一枚硬幣，搖響鈴鐺，打開門，取出御神籤並帶回。牠們也可以用喙切開繩子或膠帶來打開御神籤。


参考文献

del Hoyo, J., Elliot, A., & Christie D. (eds). (2007). Handbook of the Birds of the World. Volume 12: Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions. ISBN 978-84-96553-42-2
